what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that links the design-assets channel to an image asset via a typed target URI and a creation timestamp.
observations: The file consists entirely of YAML frontmatter; there is no prose or code body.
The target is 'image://design-assets/icons/image (59)', including a space and parentheses.
It includes an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format with microsecond precision.
standouts: Target URI contains a space and parentheses: 'image://design-assets/icons/image (59)'.
Attachment type is 'images' for the 'design-assets' channel.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable UUID id field.
References assets with a custom image:// URI scheme under a channel path.
Records an added timestamp for auditability.
concerns: Target path includes literal spaces and parentheses, which may require careful encoding when resolved.
Generic asset name 'image (59)' gives no semantic description or alt text.
No updated timestamp, author, or version fields.
notes: This is a minimal attachment ledger entry pointing the design-assets channel to a single image asset by URI.
questions: What specific icon or image does 'image (59)' represent?
Is the space and parenthesis in the target URI intentional and supported by the resolver?
Are there constraints on attachment_type values such as 'images'?
Does this attachment have a corresponding file or blob elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment record and its fields match that purpose; it records the intended link between a channel and an image asset.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to register an image asset in the design-assets channel so the channel memory system can locate and associate the image with that channel.
novelty: The target URI embeds a space and parentheses in the path, which is unusual for a clean URI reference.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, typed attachment record that maps a channel to an image asset using a URI and a creation timestamp, which a fused system could keep as a canonical reference entry.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file for the design-assets channel that references an image asset at image://design-assets/icons/image (59) and records its creation time.
intention_for_embedding: To register and persist a link between the design-assets channel and a specific image asset for later retrieval or association.
